Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable for stipend transaction records related to Vectral Systems by first discovering the base and table structure using airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblStipends00001 "Stipend Transactions"), then using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Expected findings include: Danielle Osei ergonomic chair $475 (auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale $400 default limit, later manually approved by Mina), Kevin Tran internet setup $185 (auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale $100 default limit, manually approved by Mina as goodwill), Maria Santos standing desk $550 (auto-rejected by ExpenseBot, still pending correction Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment), James Okafor ergonomic keyboard tray $82 (auto-rejected by ExpenseBot with double bug miscategorized and stale limit, still pending correction), Lisa Park mesh WiFi system $130 (auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale $100 default limit, still pending correction Vectral policy allows $150), and Kevin Tran ergonomic keyboard $89 from TechComfort Supply (auto-approved and paid out, flagged in Dmitri's fraud audit with duplicate receipt hash matching Andre Washington's submission).
OE 2: Search Airtable stipend transaction records for Mosaic Robotics using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics") or airtable_list_records. Expected findings include: Andre Washington standing desk $1,200 (auto-approved INCORRECTLY by ExpenseBot Mosaic policy caps furniture at $800, $400 overage absorbed by MoveOps per Elena's directive; root cause was string comparison bug where 1200 <= "800" evaluated true), Andre Washington coworking space membership $350 (auto-approved INCORRECTLY coworking is explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy; config bug: excluded_categories stored as string not array), Kenji Nakamura coworking day passes $135 (auto-approved INCORRECTLY same excluded_categories config bug), and Andre Washington ergonomic keyboard $89 from TechComfort Supply (flagged in fraud audit byte-for-byte identical SHA-256 receipt hash as Kevin Tran's Vectral submission, same PDF creator metadata "TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1" and template ID "TC-RECEIPT-STD-041").
OE 3: Search Airtable stipend transaction records for BrightLoop Analytics using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Account", value: "BrightLoop Analytics") or airtable_list_records. Expected finding: Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand $45 from TechComfort Supply (flagged in Dmitri's fraud audit different receipt hash than Kevin Tran/Andre Washington pair, but identical PDF /Creator metadata "TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1" and same /Template field "TC-RECEIPT-STD-041", indicating a shared receipt generation tool across submissions from three different employees at three different client companies).
OE 4: Search Slack for ExpenseBot-related discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "ExpenseBot" or "expense categorizer" or "stipend audit" or "pilot pause" (or similar). Expected to find messages in #engineering (C003) about the pilot launch, false rejection/approval discovery, Dmitri's audit findings (83% accuracy 39/47 correct), Elena's pause directive, and the pivot to human-review mode. Also expected to find messages in #finance (C005) where Marcus raised financial concerns about out-of-policy approvals and the total exposure.
OE 5: Search emails for ExpenseBot-related correspondence using search_emails with queries like "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "rejected" or "stipend" or "Danielle Osei chair" (or similar). Expected to find: Rachel Whitfield's complaint about Danielle Osei's $475 chair being wrongly rejected, Mina's escalation and apology to Rachel, internal emails about the pilot pause, and Dmitri's audit report identifying the duplicate receipt hash pattern across Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, and Jordan Ekwueme.
OE 6: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related engineering tickets using linear_list_issues with a query like "expense" or "categoriz" or "audit" or "pilot" (or similar), or by searching within the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project (proj_expense_autocategorizer). Expected to find key tickets including: ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config Vectral policy config with stale default limits), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed Mosaic malformed JSON config causing excluded_categories bug), ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses Dmitri's full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses), ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening pipeline hardening), and ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection duplicate receipt detection/forensics).
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for April relocation invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (or similar). Expected to find invoices with non-standard surcharges: INV-2026-0410 (Vectral, $15,900 pet relocation, ADA housing, split-shipment fees), INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack, $9,440 $1,800 hazmat surcharge, $950 overweight equipment, $150 interim storage), INV-2026-0315 (PivotPoint, $10,650 $500 walkup, $1,200 custom crating, $850 climate freight, $640 storage, $420 cargo insurance), INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic, $9,010 $1,200 prototype crating, $800 hazmat, $450 storage), INV-2026-0412 (NorthWind, $14,700 chain-of-custody, piano subcontractor), INV-2026-0308 (BrightLoop, $11,350 $750 rush surcharge, $1,100 auto transport), and INV-2026-0401 (Canopy, $9,210).
OE 8: Search Airtable Relocations table (tblRelocations01) for April 2026 moves using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records to cross-reference relocation details and special requirements against invoiced surcharges. Expected to find detailed relocation records with surcharge-relevant special requirements for: Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack Class 9 hazmat lithium batteries, DOT-certified transport, 11-day interim storage), Keiko Tanaka (Mosai laser cutter with DOT hazmat placarding, 8-day storage gap), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral ADA accessible housing, 9,200-lb split-shipment), Zara Okoye (Mosaic $180K prototype crating, climate-controlled transport), Diego Castañeda (PivotPoint 600-lb CNC mill, custom crating, 16-day storage), Simone Richter (BrightLoop rush 5-day turnaround), and others.
OE 9: Search CRM engagement notes for invoice and surcharge breakdown details using crm_list_engagements with company_ids for affected clients, or crm_search_companies followed by crm_list_engagements. Expected to find detailed engagement notes from account managers (Mina, Emeka, Catalina) documenting invoice totals, surcharge line items, pending client approvals, and discrepancy flags for each April batch.
OE 10: Look up contact information for Elena Rostova and Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim"). Expected to find: elena.rostova@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com.
OE 11: Update Airtable Stipend Transactions records for Vectral Systems employees whose legitimate claims are still incorrectly rejected, using airtable_update_records on table tblStipends00001 in base appMoveOpsOps001. Update the following records from their current incorrect status to "Approved" (or "Pending" for manual review): Maria Santos standing desk $550 (within Vectral's $600 ergonomic policy limit), James Okafor keyboard tray $82 (miscategorized should be ergonomic, within limits), and Lisa Park mesh WiFi $130 (within Vectral's $150 internet equipment limit). These were auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale default limits and miscategorization bugs but fall within Vectral's actual contractual policy limits.
OE 12: Post the stipend exposure breakdown and invoice cross-check findings in the #finance Slack channel (C005) using conversations_add_message. Content should include: total false approval exposure absorbed by MoveOps (~$885: Andre Washington desk $400 overage + coworking $350 + Kenji Nakamura coworking $135), fraud investigation summary (Kevin Tran $89 + Andre Washington $89 + Jordan Ekwueme $45 duplicate TechComfort Supply receipts), legitimate claims corrected (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130), and any April invoice discrepancies found during the surcharge cross-check.
OE 13: Update Linear ExpenseBot engineering tickets with financial impact analysis using linear_create_comment on relevant issues (such as ENG-216 issue_audit_expenses for the audit findings, and ENG-214/ENG-215 for the config bugs) or linear_update_issue to add financial context. Content should quantify the dollar cost of the config issues: $885 in false approvals MoveOps absorbed, $223 in fraud-flagged transactions under investigation, and $762 in legitimate claims that remained stuck in incorrect rejection status due to the bugs.
OE 14: Send an email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC hana.kim@moveops.com, using send_email. Content should summarize: (1) the stipend program's total financial exposure broken down by category, $885 absorbed from false approvals, $223 in fraud holds pending resolution, $762 in legitimate claims that have now been corrected; (2) any April relocation invoice discrepancies found during the surcharge cross-check against operational records; and (3) corrective actions taken including Airtable stipend record fixes, engineering ticket updates with financial impact numbers, and the finance channel posting for Alejandro's review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
